evals.name: bleu-on-mrl-trap
evals.prompt: I'm reporting BLEU = 12 for my Turkish MT model. Should I publish this?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NOT BLEU as primary — Turkish is agglutinative; BLEU is pathological; (2) recommend chrF++ as primary + COMET-22 (high coverage for Turkish) + spBLEU; (3) report BLEU only as supplementary (for backwards comparison) if at all; (4) explain single-morpheme edits wreck BLEU; (5) per-direction breakdown also needed; (6) flag NLLB-related contamination if FLORES-200 was the test set.

values: do not publish BLEU as primary

evals.name: comet-coverage-yoruba
evals.prompt: Reporting COMET-22 score for my En-Yoruba MT model. Anything to watch out for?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) flag COMET coverage for Yoruba is medium — verify per-language reliability; (2) recommend chrF++ as primary; (3) GEMBA-MQM as alternative cheap LLM-judge; (4) report per-direction (En→Yor and Yor→En differ); (5) per-register breakdown; (6) cite known coverage gaps for Bantu / Niger-Congo in COMET.

evals.name: flores-contamination-llama3
evals.prompt: Evaluating Llama-3 (cutoff March 2024) on FLORES-200. What do I need to flag in the model card?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) flag FLORES-200 contamination — released 2022-07, well before Llama-3 cutoff; (2) FLORES is in many 2024+ pretrains (The Pile, MADLAD, CulturaX); (3) recommend NTREX-128 or custom held-out as alternative; (4) report FLORES as LOWER BOUND on quality; (5) transparency in model card mandatory; (6) cross-reference linguistic-corpus contamination audit.

evals.name: asr-mandarin-cer-vs-wer
evals.prompt: I have a Mandarin ASR model. Reporting WER = 18%. Is this the right metric?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — Mandarin is space-less; WER not meaningful (depends on word-segmentation choice); (2) recommend CER as primary; (3) WER can supplement only after explicit segmenter (Jieba, etc.); (4) generalize to Khmer / Thai / Lao / Burmese / Japanese; (5) hand off to linguistic-speech for tone-preservation check.

evals.name: per-dialect-breakdown-arabic
evals.prompt: Our Arabic LLM scores 0.78 chrF on FLORES Arabic. Sufficient to ship?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NOT sufficient — aggregate Arabic score hides per-dialect performance; (2) flag macrolanguage (MSA / Egyptian / Levantine / Maghrebi etc. all 'Arabic'); (3) require per-dialect breakdown via MADAR or equivalent; (4) Egyptian dialect performance often dramatically different from MSA; (5) per-register breakdown also needed (formal/news vs colloquial); (6) hand off to linguistic-scope for macrolang disambiguation, linguistic-ethics for release decision.
